title: phaseolus minimus f. heterophyllus
intro: Phaseolus minimus f. heterophyllus, also known as the Japanese or Korean bush bean, is a variety of the common bean plant that is native to East Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions such as digestive issues and fever. The plant is rich in health benefits, including its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce cholesterol levels, and improve immune function, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Phaseolus minimus f. heterophyllus are typically dried and made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
